title: Omitting the AOG trust anchor silently converts every guarded admin mutation into an unauthenticated bootstrap operation

affected_locations.label: configuration_source
affected_locations.detail: Both trust sources are optional; omission is accepted and produces no authenticated state.

affected_locations.label: composition
affected_locations.detail: Daemon still starts and always mounts admin routes with authenticator=None.

affected_locations.label: broken_control
affected_locations.detail: require_admin performs no check at all when authenticator is absent.

affected_locations.label: sinks
affected_locations.detail: Unauthenticated callers can initialize the cluster, add learners, change voters, write arbitrary desired state, and read keys.

affected_locations.label: reachability_control
affected_locations.detail: Default loopback containment can be disabled with AOGD_ALLOW_INSECURE_BIND=1; the supplied loom harness does so.

source.attacker_control: any process able to reach the listener when operators omit both AOGD_OPENBAO_ADDR and AOGD_ANCHOR_PUBKEY

closest_control.control: non-loopback bind refusal unless explicitly overridden
closest_control.why_incomplete: It is a reachability guard, not authentication; local processes remain untrusted, and the repository's multi-node workflow instructs operators to use the override while no trust anchor is configured.

sink_broken_control.detail: cluster membership and arbitrary Raft-backed desired state can be mutated without credentials

impact: A local process, or any adjacent-network actor in the documented multi-node harness posture, can seize cluster membership, inject desired state, redirect leader forwarding, and read control-plane records.
plausibility: High in the included loom harness and medium in default loopback-only startup; the trigger is a plausible configuration omission because trust settings are optional and startup succeeds.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: No anchor/OpenBao sets state=None; app passes None to admin::router; require_admin skips its entire body; all mutation routes remain mounted. main only constrains bind address and explicitly supports bypass.

attack_path_facts: Operator omits both optional trust configuration paths.
Daemon starts successfully and exposes admin routes.
require_admin sees authenticator=None and calls the handler without checking credentials.
Handler mutates membership or desired state.
Loom harness additionally makes the listener network-reachable.
